mission.title: Install and Configure Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)
mission.description: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) is a powerful Kubernetes distribution that provides a developer-friendly platform for building, deploying, and managing containerized applications. It includes advanced features like integrated CI/CD, monitoring, and security, making it suitable for enterprise environments. However, it requires a subscription for full support and may have a steeper learning curve compared to other distributions.

steps.title: Install the OpenShift CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the OpenShift command-line interface (CLI) which is essential for interacting with your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/openshift-client-linux-4.17.0.tar.gz

tar -xzf openshift-client-linux-4.17.0.tar.gz

sudo mv oc kubectl /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the OpenShift CLI for version 4.17.0.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the binaries to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create an OpenShift cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new OpenShift cluster using the OpenShift installer.

```bash

openshift-install create cluster --dir=cluster --log-level=info

```

> Replace 'cluster' with your desired directory name.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to access the OpenShift cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(pwd)/cluster/auth/kubeconfig

```

> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable to point to your cluster's kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your OpenShift cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes and their statuses.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
This step configures additional features like autoscaling and monitoring.

```bash

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-autoscaler/master/deploy/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-monitoring-operator/master/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply the cluster autoscaler configuration.
> Apply the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall OpenShift cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the OpenShift cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

openshift-install destroy cluster --dir=cluster --log-level=info

```

> Ensure you have backups of any important data before proceeding.

uninstall.title: Remove orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
This step cleans up any orphaned resources that may remain after cluster deletion.

```bash

aws ec2 describe-instances --filters 'Name=tag:Name,Values=openshift-*' --query 'Reservations[*].Instances[*].InstanceId' --output text | xargs aws ec2 terminate-instances

```

> Replace with your cloud provider's commands to clean up resources.
commands: aws ec2 describe-instances --filters 'Name=tag:Name,Values=openshift-*' --query 'Reservations[*].Instances[*].InstanceId' --output text | xargs aws ec2 terminate-instances

upgrade.title: Backup cluster data
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure that you backup your cluster data.

```bash

oc adm must-gather --image=quay.io/openshift-release-dev/ocp-v4.0-art-dev:latest

```

> This command gathers cluster data for backup.

upgrade.title: Cordon and drain nodes
upgrade.description:
Prepare the nodes for upgrade by cordoning and draining them.

```bash

oc adm cordon <node-name>

oc adm drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

```

> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name.
> This command drains the node, ignoring daemonsets.

upgrade.title: Upgrade OpenShift cluster
upgrade.description:
This step performs the actual upgrade of the OpenShift cluster.

```bash

openshift-install upgrade cluster --dir=cluster --log-level=info

```

> Ensure you are in the correct directory.

upgrade.title: Verify upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the nodes after the upgrade to ensure they are ready.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes and their statuses after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the kubeconfig file is not pointing to the correct cluster or the certificate is invalid.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the KUBECONFIG environment variable is set correctly and the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no nodes available in cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the nodes are not in a ready state.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the nodes using 'oc get nodes' and troubleshoot any issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the image cannot be found or there are authentication issues.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and check if the image registry requires authentication.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster by adding more nodes or increasing the resources of existing nodes.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, enhanced security features, and improved monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for older Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 4.16, ensure that all custom resources are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'oc get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
